name: Wei Zhao (Hongsi)
page_title: Wei Zhao (Hongsi) 韋昭 (弘嗣) [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes: Wei Zhao was an historian who served Wu. He wrote the final fifty-five juan of the historic work, Wu shu (Wu History), which was Chen Shou’s primary source for Wu entries in the Sanguozhi. The Wu shu was originally a collaborative work between Zhou Zhao, Xue Ying, Ling Guang and Hua He.

sections.short-biography: Historian who completed the last fifty-five juan of the Wu shu (Wu History).

He wrote the final fifty-five juan of the historic work, Wu shu (Wu History), which was Chen Shou’s primary source for Wu entries in the Sanguozhi. full_text:
Wei Zhao (Hongsi) 韋昭 (弘嗣)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Wei Zhao (韋昭), styledHongsi (弘嗣), was born in Wu. Historian who completed the last fifty-fivejuanof theWu shu(Wu History). Wei Zhao was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Wei Zhao lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Wei Zhao was an historian who served Wu. He wrote the final fifty-fivejuanof the historic work,Wu shu(Wu History), which was Chen Shou’s primary source for Wu entries in theSanguozhi. TheWu shuwas originally a collaborative work between Zhou Zhao, Xue Ying, Ling Guang and Hua He.
